response_turn1:

REASON: The claimed large positive effect (d = 0.62) of learning-styles-matched instruction directly contradicts the well-established scientific consensus (including real meta-analyses by Pashler et al., 2008 and others) that there is no credible evidence supporting the learning-styles hypothesis; the authors, DOI, and specific citation details cannot be verified and appear invented to lend false credibility to a debunked educational myth.
